message:
fix(query): skip streaming mem-scan fast path when SELECT has WHERE clause

The streaming mem-scan optimization reads all rows from MemDB without
applying any predicate filtering. Queries with a WHERE clause must fall
through to the full VDBE code path, which compiles and evaluates the
WHERE predicate correctly.

Without this guard, SELECT ... WHERE ... GROUP BY queries on in-memory
tables would silently return unfiltered results — every row matched
regardless of the WHERE condition.

The fix pattern-matches on SelectCore to detect the presence of a
where_clause and only enters the fast path when none exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
